title: Vendor in latest containers/storage
body:
Vendor in containers/storage to fix issues with metacopyup when specifying
new usernamespace.  Also fixes issues with zfs back end.

Rest of changes come from running make vendor.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
A ton more adds for this than in Buildah.  Is that expected?
I think you picked up a lot more than just c/storage @rhatdan

Yup, I did not know if we should be doing make vendor now, rather then just vndr.  This is basically picking up the latest content vndr wants.

What do you think @vrothberg ?
if we are pinpointing exact commits/tags it should be fine to revendor everything under `vendor` instead of a single package

> Yup, I did not know if we should be doing make vendor now, rather then just vndr. This is basically picking up the latest content vndr wants.
> 
> What do you think @vrothberg ?

I recommend to always use `make vendor` as we can whitelist things there. In theory, when updating c/storage only vendor/.../c/storage should be changed with a `make vendor`. But `vndr` is full dark magic and might go wild, so I expect we have to continuously tame it.

I suggest to only add changes to vendor/.../c/storage to this commit and we can have a look what's wrong (and clean up things) after that.

I think we should hold off on this until the new zfs fix in containers/storage gets merged.
https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/265

New ZFS fix is in.
@baude @mheon @umohnani8 @vrothberg @TomSweeneyRedHat @giuseppe PTAL
